{{Chess Opening Theory/Position
|Busch-Gass gambit
|eco=[[Chess/ECOC|C40]]
|parent=[[../|Busch-Gass gambit]]}}

== 3. Nxe5 ==
White captures Black's undefended e-pawn. 

Black's plan to justify the gambit is [[/3...Qh4|'''3...Qh4''']]. This threatens mate on f2 and looks to recover the e pawn. White can counter both threats with 4. Qe2, but the most critical line is actually 4. d4! Qxe4 5. Be3, giving up the pawn but achieving a crushing lead in development (5...Nf6 6. Nd2 Qf5 7. Bd3 Qe6 8. Nxf7 Qxf7 9. dxc5).

Black's alternative third move is [[/3...Nc6|'''3...Nc6''']], the Chiodini gambit, which transposes into a Stafford gambit after 4. Nxc6 dxc6. 

The wild sacrifice [[/3...Bxf2|'''3...Bxf2+?!''']] achieves nothing. [[/3...d6|'''3...d6''']] is probably the most sound continuation, but Black has to make peace with having given up a pawn in exchange for a slightly improved [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...e5/2. Nf3/2...d6|Philidor]].
